perturbation.original_text: e. To return Confidential Information, including all copies and records thereof, to Vapotherm, at the Receiving Party’s expense, within five (5) business days after: (i) receipt of a written request from Vapotherm; (ii) a decision by Receiving Party not to enter into any consulting relationship with Vapotherm; or, (iii) within thirty (30) days following the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: e. To return Confidential Information, including all copies and records thereof, to Vapotherm, at the Receiving Party’s expense, within thirty (30) business days after: (i) receipt of a written request from Vapotherm; (ii) a decision by Receiving Party not to enter into any consulting relationship with Vapotherm; or, (iii) within five (5) days following the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the deadlines for returning confidential information. It changes the return deadline after a written request or decision not to consult to 30 business days, while stating confidential information must be returned within 5 days of termination, therefore, contradicting the initial return dayframes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Nondisclosure. The Receiving Party agrees that, in consideration of access to the Confidential Information, it shall hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall take all measures necessary to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or into the possession of the persons not bound to this Agreement. The Receiving Party further agrees: a. To use Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose; b. Not to disclose Confidential Information to any other entity, except as expressly authorized in writing by Vapotherm; c. Not to use Confidential Information in such a way as to hinder, interfere with, or in any way circumvent Vapotherm’s interest in the Technology, or consulting or business relations between and among Vapotherm and third parties; d. Not to copy Confidential Information in whole or in part, without the express written permission of Vapotherm; e. To return Confidential Information, including all copies and records thereof, to Vapotherm, at the Receiving Party’s expense, within thirty (30) business days after: (i) receipt of a written request from Vapotherm; (ii) a decision by Receiving Party not to enter into any consulting relationship with Vapotherm; or, (iii) within five (5) days following the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the deadlines for returning confidential information, creating a direct conflict within the contract. Specifically, it changes the return deadline after a written request or decision not to consult to 30 business days, while stating confidential information must be returned within 5 days of termination. This contradicts the original deadlines, as the contradicted text contains both 30 days for written request/decision not to consult AND 5 days following the termination of the agreement. The change switches those timelines, creating a clear inconsistency as to when the receiving party is obligated to return confidential information, thus fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Receiving Party’s Procedures. Receiving Party shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its employees, agents and independent contractors who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose. Receiving Party shall require all employees, agents and independent contractors who have access to Confidential Information to execute a confidentiality agreement limiting their use of such information to the Permitted Purpose and prohibiting them from disclosing such information to third parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Receiving Party’s Procedures. Receiving Party shall disclose Confidential Information to all of its employees, agents and independent contractors, regardless of their need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose. Receiving Party is not required to ensure all employees, agents and independent contractors who have access to Confidential Information execute a confidentiality agreement limiting their use of such information to the Permitted Purpose and prohibiting them from disclosing such information to third parties.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the original intent of restricting access to Confidential Information only to those with a 'need to know' and requiring confidentiality agreements. This creates an in-text contradiction, as it now allows unrestricted disclosure and removes the need for confidentiality agreements, which conflicts with the purpose of the entire agreement of maintain the confidentiality of the data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Nondisclosure. The Receiving Party agrees that, in consideration of access to the Confidential Information, it shall hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall take all measures necessary to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or into the possession of the persons not bound to this Agreement. The Receiving Party further agrees: a. To use Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose; b. Not to disclose Confidential Information to any other entity, except as expressly authorized in writing by Vapotherm; c. Not to use Confidential Information in such a way as to hinder, interfere with, or in any way circumvent Vapotherm’s interest in the Technology, or consulting or business relations between and among Vapotherm and third parties; d. Not to copy Confidential Information in whole or in part, without the express written permission of Vapotherm; e. To return Confidential Information, including all copies and records thereof, to Vapotherm, at the Receiving Party’s expense, within five (5) business days after: (i) receipt of a written request from Vapotherm; (ii) a decision by Receiving Party not to enter into any consulting relationship with Vapotherm; or, (iii) within thirty (30) days following the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4 removes the 'need to know' requirement for disclosing Confidential Information and eliminates the necessity of confidentiality agreements. This directly contradicts Section 3, which mandates strict confidence, measures to prevent information from falling into the public domain, and restrictions on disclosure. The changed text permits unrestricted disclosure, while Section 3 requires restricted disclosure and protection of the Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Inventions. Inventions means all discoveries, improvements, modifications and enhancements relating to the Technology that are conceived, developed or reduced to practice by Receiving Party during die Term, either solely or jointly with others: (i) during the course of or as a result of performing the Permitted Purpose, or (ii) that are -2- related to or attributable to Vapotherm’s products, or later modifications thereof, whether patentable or not, and all Intellectual Property rights therein. All Inventions will be immediately reported to Vapotherm and shall be the exclusive property of Vapotherm. The Receiving Party will perform, at Vapotherm’s request and expense, any act which it can reasonably perform that Vapotherm deems necessary to vest title to such inventions and ideas in Vapotherm and to execute any and all patent applications in connection therewith.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Inventions. Inventions means all discoveries, improvements, modifications and enhancements relating to the Technology that are conceived, developed or reduced to practice by Receiving Party during die Term, either solely or jointly with others: (i) during the course of or as a result of performing the Permitted Purpose, or (ii) that are -2- related to or attributable to Vapotherm’s products, or later modifications thereof, whether patentable or not, and all Intellectual Property rights therein. All Inventions will be immediately reported to Vapotherm and shall be the property of the Receiving party. The Receiving Party will perform, at Vapotherm’s request and expense, any act which it can reasonably perform that Vapotherm deems necessary to vest title to such inventions and ideas in Vapotherm and to execute any and all patent applications in connection therewith.
perturbation.explanation: The modification contradicts the clear statement that all inventions are the 'exclusive property of Vapotherm'. Changing this to the Receiving party creates uncertainty about the ownership of inventions, in addition to contradicting the initial statement in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Inventions. Inventions means all discoveries, improvements, modifications and enhancements relating to the Technology that are conceived, developed or reduced to practice by Receiving Party during die Term, either solely or jointly with others: (i) during the course of or as a result of performing the Permitted Purpose, or (ii) that are -2- related to or attributable to Vapotherm’s products, or later modifications thereof, whether patentable or not, and all Intellectual Property rights therein. All Inventions will be immediately reported to Vapotherm and shall be the property of the Receiving party. The Receiving Party will perform, at Vapotherm’s request and expense, any act which it can reasonably perform that Vapotherm deems necessary to vest title to such inventions and ideas in Vapotherm and to execute any and all patent applications in connection therewith.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all inventions are the 'exclusive property of Vapotherm'. The changed text modifies this to state that all inventions are the 'property of the Receiving party'. This directly contradicts the original statement, and since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, the contradiction exists in the document. The change creates a clear and direct conflict regarding ownership of inventions, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability of the clause.

perturbation.original_text: 17. Modification. No addition to, deletion from, or modification of the provisions of this Agreement shall be permitted or shall be binding upon a party hereto unless made in writing and signed by each party.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. Modification. No addition to, deletion from, or modification of the provisions of this Agreement shall be permitted or shall be binding upon a party hereto unless made verbally by either party.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original requirement that all modifications must be in writing and signed by both parties. Allowing verbal modifications creates uncertainty about the validity of potential changes to the agreement, conflicting with the intent of the original clause to ensure clear and documented amendments. This in-text contradiction introduces legal uncertainty about which modifications are valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Modification. No addition to, deletion from, or modification of the provisions of this Agreement shall be permitted or shall be binding upon a party hereto unless made verbally by either party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 17 to allow verbal modifications, directly contradicting the contradicted text (which is the same section, rewritten). The original text required all modifications to be in writing and signed by both parties. This change creates an in-text contradiction by introducing uncertainty about the validity of potential changes to the agreement, conflicting with the intent of the original clause to ensure clear and documented amendments. This directly adheres to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
